bullets.text: Fed/OCC findings clear Morgan Stanley Bank for a Section 23A exemption, easing limits on affiliate transactions and liquidity management.
DHS funding lapse becomes longest partial shutdown; Trump vows executive action to pay TSA agents, reducing airport disruptions but highlighting fiscal brinkmanship.
Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz pressures shipping and energy; limited tanker transit keeps crude risk premium elevated, with OECD warning global spillovers.
Trump extends pause on strikes on Iranian energy facilities to April 6; equities slide into correction, suggesting investors discount de-escalation messaging.
Israel says it killed Iran’s naval chief overseeing the Hormuz blockade, raising escalation odds and potential supply shocks.
China industrial profits rise 15% early-year, but officials and analysts flag oil-price shocks as a key downside risk to manufacturing margins.
A forecasting group projects U.S. inflation at 4.2% in 2026, above Fed estimates; political fights over Fed leadership add rate-path uncertainty.
AI data-center buildout faces local permitting backlash and looming power scrutiny; Meta’s $10B Texas project highlights grid, nuclear-fuel, and labor constraints.
Court grants Anthropic a preliminary injunction blocking a Pentagon 'supply chain risk' label, citing possible retaliation—important precedent for AI procurement and speech.
Fannie Mae accepts its first crypto-backed mortgage product, signaling mainstreaming of digital assets in housing finance while introducing valuation and collateral-liquidity risks.
Wall Street bonuses total $49.2B, underscoring strong bank profitability and a near-term boost to New York tax receipts despite volatile markets.
Meta shares fall nearly 8% after two court defeats; Netflix raises prices across plans—contrasting legal overhangs with continued consumer-subscription pricing power.
Deepfake X-rays now look real even to doctors, amplifying risks of medical fraud, misdiagnosis, and the need for provenance and detection standards.
Researchers identify an immune cell linked to long-COVID fatigue, offering a potential biomarker and therapeutic target for persistent symptoms.
